GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "An ingestible beauty pioneer on how she got her start in business", "The Torrens University Australia graduate has been championing the idea of beauty, from the inside, for decades.", "It's a booming global business now, but back in the '90s, when herbalist and founder of Tea Tonic, Lisa Hilbert, blended her first signature herbal tea, Complexion Tea, the concoction was a true innovation. The careful combination of organic cleansing herbs, which together help support healthy, glowing skin, is still one of the naturopath's best-selling formulas more than 20 years later. (Never one to rest, Tea Tonic also has 25 other all-natural tea remedies and mixtures). Hilbert, Australia's only female tea maker, studied naturopathy at Southern School of Natural Therapies (SSNT) at Torrens University's Melbourne campus (the University also has schools across Sydney, Adelaide, Brisbane and online too, where students can study areas like nutrition, counselling and community service, public health, beauty and dermal therapies, as well as sports development). She says she was destined for a career in natural therapies. "I wanted to be a naturopath since I was 14. When I was a child, I had treatment for things like asthma and eczema, so I had a naturopath, and I saw how valuable the fundamentals of naturopathy are and how effective herbs could be," she explains. While she set up a clinical practice following graduation from SSNT at Torrens University, Hilbert, who now works full-time in her Melbourne tea business, also saw how just how effective naturopathy could be, when made highly accessible. "Through Tea Tonic, I've seen what a positive influence on a person's lifestyle and health habits I can have, purely through a simple cup of tea," she says. In her studies, Lisa experienced first-hand not just a focus on equipping students with the knowledge they needed to pass the course, but on building a globally orientated skill-set, to better prepare them for all-round success. "Everything I studied there has been relevant. It's not stuff where you think 'I'm never going to use this.' Even the chemistry! I have a herbal tea business, but I can read scientific studies into ingredients and how they work, and I can understand it, because of my education. I still have all of my uni books—I won't throw them away," laughs Hilbert. Torrens University also boasts an impressive list of teachers and collaborators who have years of practical experience in their respective subjects. "The teachers at Torrens University are phenomenal. They're not just teachers—they're practitioners with deep experience in the field. They know their craft. You get such a different level of information from teachers who also practice in a clinic," she says. Tea Tonic is now stocked all over, including in some of Australia's pre-eminent function venues, but Hilbert says it's her commitment to quality that she is most proud of. While everyone was wrapping their tea bags in cellophane, Hilbert packed Tea Tonic's unbleached filter paper-wrapped tea in paper envelopes, without staples and entirely compostable, so it was better for the drinker, and better for the planet. Her Torrens University course set her off in a trajectory that is still rocketing today, decades after she completed it. "It's an exciting career path to take. There are so many fields where training in health is required. And I think it's only growing. It's knowledge that you can use in so many ways—anything is possible. It's a real job of the future," Hilbert says. To hear more from Lisa Hilbert and other prominent Torrens University Australia graduates, register for their Virtual Open Day on the 11th of August here."
